This sheet calculates your mortgage for your income statement
The Amortization Schedule is a tool designed to assist in evaluating various mortgage scenarios in an
efficient fashion.  It requires four basic inputs:
This is the amount that will be mortgaged and can be derived by taking lending value of the property
multiplied by the Loan to Value percentage.  These can be calculated mannually or derived from the
This is the number of years that the mortgage is payable for.  This may or may not be the same as the
This is the interest rate of the mortgage expressed as a percentage.
This is the number of years that the prinicpal and interest on the mortgage are amortized over for purposes
of arriving at the monthly payment that will be due.  In cases where the term of the loan is shorter than the
amortization, a balloon payment is calculated on the sheet and the total interest is calculated through the
Using these four inputs, the Amortization Schedule will calculate the annual, monthly, and weekly debt
service payment for the contemplated mortgage and will return the total cost of the mortgage, as well as
any balloon payment, in rows 10 an 11.
